blocks.text: Fernhollow Partners LLC
Amanda Golden, VP Finance, Nguyen, Reynolds and Martinez
Re: Transaction Readiness Review
Dear Ms. Golden:

This letter confirms that {{fact:f:E-2022-001.client}} has engaged Fernhollow Partners LLC to conduct a transaction readiness review of Nguyen, Reynolds and Martinez. The work begins {{fact:f:E-2022-001.start}} and runs approximately seven months, through delivery of a written readiness assessment and a briefing to your board. We are glad the working relationship continues in your new seat, and we intend to keep earning it.

The purpose of the review is plain: when a buyer or lender arrives, your numbers should already answer the questions. We will assemble and organize the data room, perform a quality-of-numbers review of the financial statements and supporting schedules, and prepare management for diligence itself. Document needs come to you in a single request list at kickoff. We do not opine on legal, tax, or accounting matters, and we do not negotiate on your behalf. The deliverables are an organized data room, a readiness assessment with remediation items ranked by importance, and a short briefing suitable for board use.

Sandra Perez, Director, leads the engagement day to day and is your first call. Briana Dennis, Senior Associate, runs the quality-of-numbers review and drafts the assessment. Ryan Strong, Analyst, builds the data room index and ties the schedules to source. I read the assessment and the remediation log before either reaches you.

Our fee for this scope is {{fact:f:E-2022-001.fee}}. The fee is fixed and covers professional time and routine expenses. Unusual expenses require your written approval before we incur them. Invoices are issued monthly and are payable on receipt. Should the scope change, we agree the adjustment in writing before additional work begins.

We hold your information in confidence. Materials you provide are used only for this engagement and are returned or destroyed at your direction when the work concludes. Our workpapers remain our property.
If these terms are acceptable, please sign below and return one copy to my attention. Until then, questions can go to Sandra Perez or to me directly. We look forward to the work.
Kickoff Memo: Transaction Readiness Review for Nguyen, Reynolds and Martinez
To: Briana Dennis; Ryan Strong. From: Sandra Perez. Re: readiness review kickoff.

The engagement letter is countersigned. {{fact:f:E-2022-001.client}} has engaged us for a transaction readiness review, effective {{fact:f:E-2022-001.start}} and running roughly seven months. The objective is a company that walks into diligence with nothing left to explain: a data room a buyer can navigate without asking, figures that tie to source, and a management team that has already heard the hard questions. Amanda Golden, VP Finance, is our client lead. She has worked with us before and knows how we run, so route every request through her and copy me.
Workstreams

items: Data room. Ryan builds the index and loads batches as they arrive; the structure mirrors the request list so gaps show themselves.
Quality of numbers. Briana owns the review of the annual financials and supporting schedules, every figure tied to source.
Remediation log. Briana keeps the ranked list of items to fix before outside eyes see the room; I review it weekly.
Management preparation. I run the diligence-question sessions with Amanda Golden's team once the room is substantially loaded.

blocks.text: Next Steps

Request list to Amanda by Friday. Ryan stands up the workbook and the data room skeleton this week; first internal check-in next Tuesday. Bring gaps, not polish. On readiness work the client pays us to find problems before a buyer does, so finding one means the job is going well.
Amanda Golden, Briana Dennis, Ryan Strong:

A short status on the {{fact:f:E-2022-001.client}} readiness review before the midweek calls. The data room index is complete and the first two document batches are loaded. Briana's quality-of-numbers review is through the annual statements, and the remediation log is ranked, short, and mostly documentation rather than substance.

Two asks on the Nguyen, Reynolds and Martinez side. Amanda, we need the remaining lease and contract schedules Ryan requested, and thirty minutes with you this week to walk the open items together. With both in hand we hold the briefing date we discussed.
Nothing above is off plan. I will write again after we walk the log.
Sandra Perez
Transaction Readiness Review: Nguyen, Reynolds and Martinez
Midpoint client briefing prepared for {{fact:f:E-2022-001.client}} by Fernhollow Partners LLC. Presented by Sandra Perez, Director.
Engagement Snapshot

items: Readiness review under way since {{fact:f:E-2022-001.start}}
Scope: data room build, quality of numbers, diligence preparation
Client lead: Amanda Golden, VP Finance
Seven-month program, now past its midpoint

blocks.text: Progress Since Kickoff

items: Data room index complete; core document batches loaded (Ryan Strong)
Quality-of-numbers review through the annual statements (Briana Dennis)
Remediation log established, ranked by importance, and shrinking
No finding to date that would move a buyer's view of the numbers

blocks.text: Open Items

items: Remaining lease and contract schedules from the finance team
Management diligence-question sessions to be scheduled
Final pass on customer and supplier documentation

blocks.text: Next Steps

items: Close remaining remediation items with Amanda Golden's team
Run the management preparation sessions
Deliver the written readiness assessment
Confirm timing for the closing board briefing

blocks.text: Meeting Minutes: Transaction Readiness Review for Nguyen, Reynolds and Martinez
Working session on the {{fact:f:E-2022-001.client}} readiness engagement, held by video conference. Minutes prepared by Briana Dennis; corrections by Friday, after which these stand as written.
Attendees

items: Sandra Perez, Director, Fernhollow Partners LLC
Briana Dennis, Senior Associate, Fernhollow Partners LLC
Ryan Strong, Analyst, Fernhollow Partners LLC
Amanda Golden, VP Finance, Nguyen, Reynolds and Martinez

blocks.text: Discussion

Sandra Perez opened on the remediation log. The ranked items are down to a short tail, and nothing remaining touches the core financial statements. Ryan Strong walked through the state of the data room: the index is stable, and the latest document batch from the client loaded cleanly against it. Amanda Golden confirmed her team can turn the last schedule requests within the week.

The back half of the session covered management preparation. Amanda Golden asked that the diligence-question sessions run before her quarter-end close, and Sandra Perez agreed to compress the schedule rather than split it. The written readiness assessment will follow the sessions, so its findings reflect how management actually answers, not only what the room contains.
Action Items

items: Amanda Golden: return the remaining schedule requests to Ryan Strong within the week
Sandra Perez: circulate the compressed plan for the management preparation sessions
Ryan Strong: freeze the data room index and log any late additions separately
Briana Dennis: outline the readiness assessment so drafting starts the day the sessions end
